perturbation.original_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, whichever occurs first, I will surrender promptlyto the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoeverthat is in my possession.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, I will surrender promptly to the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoever that is in my possession within 30 days. (2) However, for subcontractors, the surrender of PCII must occur immediately upon the termination of their subcontract.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the timeframe for surrendering PCII. Originally, the surrender was expected 'promptly', meaning without delay. The modified text specifies a 30-day window for employees and consultants, but demands immediate surrender for subcontractors. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which deadline applies to subcontractors, thus contradicting the initial broad instruction and creating conflicting obligations. This directly impacts section 5(a)(1) and creates conflict with original definition of promptly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, I will surrender promptly to the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoever that is in my possession within 30 days. (2) However, for subcontractors, the surrender of PCII must occur immediately upon the termination of their subcontract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original requirement of 'promptly' surrendering PCII upon completion of engagement. It introduces a 30-day window for employees/consultants but demands immediate surrender for subcontractors. This creates a direct contradiction because the 'promptly' timeframe is no longer universally applicable, and there are now two conflicting deadlines (30 days vs. immediately) for similar obligations (surrendering PCII), which directly contradicts the original text in section 5(a)(1). The contradicted text is the exact same section, which is modified.

perturbation.original_text: 8. If I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, such violation may result in the cancellation of my conditional access to the information covered by this Agreement. This may serve as a basis for denying me conditional access to othertypes of information, to include classified national security information.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. If I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, such violation will automatically result in the cancellation of my conditional access to the information covered by this Agreement. This will serve as the sole basis for denying me conditional access to other types of information, to include classified national security information. However, violations related to SBU will be subject to a review process.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that a violation 'may result' in cancellation of access, giving DHS discretion. The modified text states that a violation 'will automatically result' in cancellation, removing discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction as it then introduces the phrase 'However, violations related to SBU will be subject to a review process.', adding back discretion only for SBU violations, thus contradicting the 'automatic' cancellation clause for SBU violations. This affects the enforcement of violation consequences, creating uncertainty about the actual penalty. The contradiction lies within Section 8 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. If I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, such violation will automatically result in the cancellation of my conditional access to the information covered by this Agreement. This will serve as the sole basis for denying me conditional access to other types of information, to include classified national security information. However, violations related to SBU will be subject to a review process.
perturbation.justification: The changed text within Section 8 initially states that a violation 'will automatically result' in cancellation of access, removing discretion. However, it then introduces an exception for SBU violations, which are 'subject to a review process'. This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 8 itself because it simultaneously mandates automatic cancellation and allows for a review process (discretion) for specific violations. This inconsistency impacts the enforcement of penalties and creates uncertainty about the consequences of violations, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me bythis Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter, except for obligations related to SBU information, which expire one year after my access is terminated.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all conditions and obligations apply 'at all times thereafter'. The modified text adds an exception: obligations related to SBU information expire one year after access is terminated. This creates an in-text contradiction because one part of the sentence says obligations continue indefinitely, while another part says they expire after a year (specifically for SBU). This uncertainty affects the duration of obligations and creates potential disputes regarding compliance after one year. The contradiction is contained within Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter, except for obligations related to SBU information, which expire one year after my access is terminated.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all conditions and obligations apply 'at all times thereafter'. The modified text introduces an exception for obligations related to SBU information, which expire one year after access termination. This creates a direct conflict within Section 11, as one part states indefinite continuation of obligations, while the other states expiration after one year for SBU information. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation type. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location. This is permissible because the contradiction exists due to the modifications introduced.

perturbation.original_text: 9. (a) With respect to SSI and SBU, I hereby assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of the informationnot consistent with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. (a) With respect to SSI, I hereby assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of the information not consistent with the terms of this Agreement. However, any assignment of rights related to SBU information requires a separate written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text assigns all royalties related to SSI and SBU to the government. The modified text keeps this for SSI but adds that SBU assignment requires a 'separate written agreement.' This creates a direct contradiction. The first part of the modified text implies automatic assignment for SBU, while the second part demands a separate agreement, creating uncertainty about the actual assignment process and contradicting the implication that SBU and SSI assignments are treated the same. The contradiction occurs within Section 9(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. (a) With respect to SSI, I hereby assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of the information not consistent with the terms of this Agreement. However, any assignment of rights related to SBU information requires a separate written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9(a) to treat SSI and SBU differently. The original text implies that royalties for both SSI and SBU are automatically assigned to the US government. The changed text states that while royalties for SSI are automatically assigned, SBU royalties require a separate written agreement. Since the changed text includes both the original assignment language AND a caveat that SBU needs a separate agreement within the same Section 9(a), it creates a direct contradiction within the same section, triggering the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization. Should situations arise that warrant the disclosure or release of such information I will do soonly under approved circumstances and in accordance with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable tothe specific categories of information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization. Should situations arise that warrant the disclosure or release of such information I will do soonly under approved circumstances and in accordance with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable tothe specific categories of information. However, disclosures of SBU information to immediate family members are permitted, regardless of authorization.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure without proper authority. The modified text adds an exception: disclosure of SBU information to immediate family is permitted, regardless of authorization. This creates an in-text contradiction. The first part prohibits unauthorized disclosure, while the second part permits it under certain circumstances (SBU to family). This undermines the original non-disclosure obligation and generates confusion over permissible disclosures. The contradiction lies within Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization. Should situations arise that warrant the disclosure or release of such information I will do soonly under approved circumstances and in accordance with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable tothe specific categories of information. However, disclosures of SBU information to immediate family members are permitted, regardless of authorization.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 4, which is also the contradicted text. The original text prohibits disclosure of information without authorization. The changed text adds an exception, allowing disclosure of SBU information to immediate family members regardless of authorization. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 4 itself, where one part prohibits unauthorized disclosure, and another part permits it, thus contradicting the original non-disclosure obligation.
